references.title: Multi-Granularity Ensemble Interaction Graph Modeling for Knowledge Tracing

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Probability of answering next exercise correctly.
extracted.Students: 4217 ASSISTments2009 learners, 1709 ASSISTments 2017 learners, 5000 EdNet learners, 118971 Eedi learners
extracted.Data sources: Sequences of Answers, Question Sentences, LMS logs
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models:
KT approaches often consider different knowledge concepts as independent from one another, which is unrealistic. 

Graph-based KT methods have started to introduce relationships between concepts to update learner knowledge.

However, the way the graph is built does not really consider changes in knowledge state for either dynamic or static perspectives.

Moreover, learning encompasses different temporal resolutions, something that current models do not seem to completely grasp
extracted.Novelty of Model:
Model uses a Transformer Knowledge State encoder to represent learning at different temporal resolutions - days, weeks, months, semesters.

Moreover, the authors introduce an Interaction Graph Dependency Module which that builds 2 knowledge graphs and uses them to build a more stable mapping of correlations between concepts.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Model starts with the construction of 3 embeddings - Questions, Knowledge Concepts, and Answers

The transformer for Knowledge States does a separate normalization of rows and columns to expedite training time.

Graph modules are built by measuring dissimarity between nodes of the graphs. A connection is etablished if similarity is above a specified threshold. One of the graphs is passed through a low-pass (smoothes the edges) filter while the other is passed by a high-pass filter (sharpens the edges). 

The results are concated.
extracted.Models: DKT, DKVMN, SAKT, MRTKT, DTransformer, GKT, DGEKT, MGIGKT
extracted.Comments:
Authors introduce a new model meant solve 2 problems:

1 - Consider different temporal resolutions of the learning process

2 - Encode relationships between different knowledge concepts to provide a better estimate of learners.

Some of the preprocessing decisions are made in order to reduce the training time of the model.

In addition, authors perform an ablation study and notice to check the impact of presence/absence of each of the proposed modules proposed.

annotations.text: KT analyzes historical records to infer students’ knowledge states, and deep learning methods are the current state-of-the-art. However, the current techniques tend to treat records as a single time-series AND they don’t account for possible correlations.

Authors propose Multi-Granularity Ensemble Interaction Graph Modeling for Knowledge Tracing which is meant to address the two constraints. Analysis on four datasets show that the model outperforms other approaches.
